agency_name: Department of Agriculture, Fisheries and Forestry (DAFF)
executive_summary: DAFF is a large, regionally-distributed (6,900 staff, 159 offices) policy-and-regulatory department operating as a hybrid of policy department and national regulator (biosecurity, exports, levies). Its 2025-26 agenda is dominated by three strategic objectives — sector growth/trade, sector resilience and sustainability, and national biosecurity — underpinned by a multi-year Transformation Action Plan (TAP) and a 10-year digital investment roadmap that includes two flagship system modernisations (Simplified Targeting and Enhanced Processing System; Digital Services to Take Farmers to Markets) plus a 4-year ICT/security/asset refresh. Significant latent demand sits in digital/ICT delivery, regulatory maturity, program evaluation, data analytics, cost-recovery/financial management uplift, and emergency-management capability — several of which are reinforced by external pressure (an ANAO cost-recovery audit, an ANAO cultural-reform audit, industry calls for an independent audit of DAFF expenses, and a biosecurity levy controversy). Recent Secretary turnover adds strategic-direction uncertainty. The APS Strategic Commissioning Framework will constrain core-work outsourcing, so the firm should pitch specialised, time-bound advisory, assurance and build/implementation work rather than bodies-in-seats.

strategic_priorities.title: Modernising trade and biosecurity ICT systems
strategic_priorities.description: DAFF is delivering two flagship digital programs — the Simplified Targeting and Enhanced Processing System and Digital Services to Take Farmers to Markets — plus a 4-year security, technology and asset refresh, all under a 10-year digital investment and sustainment roadmap to address ICT technical debt and cyber risk.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: We are modernising Australia's import and export systems through 2 flagship programs... underpinned by a strengthened enterprise data capability.

strategic_priorities.title: Improving regulatory service delivery and maturity
strategic_priorities.description: A TAP priority for 2025-26 is to improve regulatory service delivery, lift enterprise regulatory maturity under RMG 128, and strengthen regulatory capability and culture across exports, biosecurity, imported food and levies.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: improving our regulatory service delivery to stakeholders

strategic_priorities.title: Embedding an enterprise approach to program evaluation
strategic_priorities.description: DAFF is embedding an enterprise-wide program evaluation approach, with monitoring/evaluation/learning explicitly noted as 'underway' for major programs such as the Future Drought Fund and Climate-Smart Agriculture Program.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: embedding an enterprise approach to program evaluation

strategic_priorities.title: Strengthening data analytics and enterprise data maturity
strategic_priorities.description: Continuing to uplift data maturity through a new enterprise data strategy to support intelligence-led biosecurity, export operations and trade market access, and to improve corporate insight and performance.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: continuing to uplift our data maturity through our new enterprise data strategy

strategic_priorities.title: Biosecurity 2030 and emergency-management preparedness
strategic_priorities.description: Implementing the DAFF Biosecurity 2030 Roadmap and National Biosecurity Strategy, becoming intelligence-led, and strengthening emergency-management capability (e.g. H5 bird flu preparedness) against a measured maturity baseline.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: The DAFF Biosecurity 2030 Roadmap outlines our vision for a stronger and more integrated biosecurity system.

strategic_priorities.title: Enterprise financial management reform
strategic_priorities.description: A TAP priority 'in progress' to develop an enterprise-wide approach to financial management, uplift budget maturity via a forward-looking strategic roadmap, and manage cost-recovery arrangements under the Charging Framework.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Develop an enterprise-wide approach to financial management (in progress).

strategic_priorities.title: Climate-smart agriculture and net-zero transition
strategic_priorities.description: Delivering the $302.1m Climate-Smart Agriculture Program, the Future Drought Fund, and the Agriculture and Land Sector Plan, with international engagement on emissions-reduction pathways.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: We are supporting the adoption of climate-smart and sustainable agricultural practices through the $302.1 million Climate-Smart Agriculture Program.

reform_and_change_drivers: APS Reform agenda and the Transformation Action Plan (TAP) published Dec 2023, with 2025-26 focus on strategic policy, regulatory service delivery, evaluation and data maturity
APS Strategic Commissioning Framework requiring reduced outsourcing of core work in administration, accounting/finance, data/research, ICT/digital and program/project management
10-year digital investment and sustainment roadmap plus 4-year security, technology and asset refresh, governed by a PMO with reporting to Finance and the DTA
Cost-recovery and charging pressure under the Australian Government Charging Framework (enterprise risk 5; consolidated levies measure SG-02)
Biosecurity 2030 Roadmap and National Biosecurity Strategy Action Plan 2024 driving intelligence-led, technology-enabled biosecurity
Climate risk: whole-of-government Climate Risk and Opportunity Management Program and annual Commonwealth climate disclosure
Geopolitical/trade volatility (new US tariffs, shifting global trade policy) affecting export market access
Emerging biosecurity threats (e.g. H5 highly pathogenic avian influenza) increasing emergency-management demands
Workforce attrition, ageing workforce and competitive labour market driving Workforce and Recruitment Strategies 2024-27
challenges_and_risks: Nine enterprise risks named, including digital service-delivery capability, financial resources, regulatory obligations/outcomes, workforce capability, and security/critical infrastructure (cyber)
ICT technical debt and risk of system failures, explicitly driving the asset/security refresh
Cost-recovery sustainability and transparency, with industry distrust externally evident
Reliance on co-design and external delivery partners (states, RDCs, universities, NGOs) creating program-timing and expenditure risk for FDF and Climate-Smart programs
Weak/maturing performance measures: several measures newly introduced, qualitative, or flagged as 'to be reviewed', and a stated intent to develop outcome-oriented measures
Constraint on outsourcing core work under the Strategic Commissioning Framework, narrowing the kinds of engagements the firm can win
Leadership transition (Secretary departure reported externally) creating strategic-direction uncertainty
Cyber, data and personnel security risks under the Protective Security Policy Framework

news_and_controversies.summary: DAFF's proposed importer-/producer-funded biosecurity (protection) levy became a significant sectoral controversy, with industry funding arrangements under active scrutiny by an agriculture panel.
news_and_controversies.implication: Biosecurity funding redesign, cost-recovery modelling and contested stakeholder engagement is a live workstream — advisory opportunity, though politically sensitive.

news_and_controversies.summary: The Australian Meat Industry Council (AMIC) publicly called for an independent audit of DAFF's expenses, signalling industry distrust of the department's cost-recovery/charging.
news_and_controversies.implication: Cost-recovery transparency and charging methodology is a pressure point — scope for independent financial review and stakeholder-confidence work.

news_and_controversies.summary: Reporting indicates DAFF Secretary Adam Fennessy departed the role, with a substantial (~$900k) payout reported.
news_and_controversies.implication: Leadership turnover at the top creates strategic-direction uncertainty and potential re-prioritisation that the firm should track for engagement timing.

news_and_controversies.summary: DAFF was reported as not having been consulted by Treasury before the Federal Budget's agriculture changes, indicating a coordination/policy-ownership tension.
news_and_controversies.implication: Policy fragmentation between Treasury and the portfolio creates gaps where impact-analysis and implementation-readiness advice is valuable.

anao_findings.summary: ANAO performance audit 'Cost recovery in the Department of Agriculture, Fisheries and Forestry' (2024) examined DAFF's cost-recovery arrangements (specific findings not verified in research).
anao_findings.implication: Directly reinforces industry distrust of charging; likely recommendations on charging transparency and financial management — strong demand signal for financial-sustainability review and assurance.

anao_findings.summary: ANAO performance audit on DAFF's cultural reform program (specific findings not verified in research).
anao_findings.implication: Signals governance/culture remediation; opportunity in change management, governance uplift and workforce reform.

anao_findings.summary: ANAO performance audit on compliance with domestic and international travel requirements in DAFF (specific findings not verified in research).
anao_findings.implication: Points to internal-controls/probity scrutiny; scope for compliance and assurance support.

opportunity_areas.area: Delivery and assurance of flagship trade/biosecurity systems
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Two named flagship programs (Simplified Targeting and Enhanced Processing System; Digital Services to Take Farmers to Markets) plus a 4-year ICT refresh must modernise import/export systems and reduce technical debt.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Discovery and service design, systems development/integration and implementation support for the export/biosecurity platforms, scaled to each program; coupled with business and process analysis to streamline regulatory workflows.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan explicitly names both programs, a 10-year digital roadmap and a dedicated PMO reporting to Finance and the DTA — clear, funded, time-bound demand. Enterprise risk 7 (digital service-delivery capability) reinforces this. The firm can build, not just advise.

opportunity_areas.area: Independent assurance of major digital/ICT program delivery
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Large, multi-year digital programs governed by a PMO and project board carry delivery and benefits-realisation risk.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Independent gateway-style delivery assurance and program health checks at key stages, plus a benefits-management framework, for the flagship system programs and the asset/security refresh.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Robust governance is explicitly cited as critical to the refresh program; enterprise risk 7 and the scale of the digital roadmap make independent assurance credible. Pairs with the Digital & ICT delivery opportunity.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Cost-recovery arrangements are under industry and ANAO scrutiny; an enterprise approach to financial management and budget-maturity uplift is a TAP priority 'in progress'.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Activity-based costing model refresh and a financial-sustainability/charging review across export, biosecurity and levies cost-recovery, aligned to the Charging Framework, with transparent methodology to rebuild stakeholder confidence.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Enterprise risk 5 and SG-02 (levies cost recovery, target under review in 2026-27) speak directly to this; an ANAO cost-recovery audit (2024) and AMIC's call for an independent audit of expenses are strong external demand signals.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: DAFF is embedding an enterprise approach to program evaluation, with MEL 'underway' for FDF and Climate-Smart Agriculture and several measures flagged as not yet outcome-oriented.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Design of a PGPA-aligned enterprise evaluation framework plus targeted process/outcome evaluations of major programs (FDF, Climate-Smart Agriculture, emissions-reduction measures).
opportunity_areas.rationale: Explicit TAP priority; the plan repeatedly notes measures will be revised toward outcomes and that MEL work is underway — clear, named demand across multiple large programs.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: DAFF consolidated key activities from 21 to 8 and reworked measures; several are newly introduced, qualitative, proxy, or 'to be reviewed/confirmed', with a stated aim for a balanced, outcome-oriented mix.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A PGPA-aligned performance-measure review to strengthen effectiveness/efficiency measures and develop outcome-oriented indicators, with supporting data-source assessment.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan's own materiality policy, measure changes and explicit intent to develop outcome-oriented measures (e.g. RS-04, BI-05) signal a live framework-uplift need.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Strengthening data analytics is a TAP priority 'in progress' under a new enterprise data strategy, supporting intelligence-led biosecurity, trade and corporate performance.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Information/data strategy support, business intelligence build, and analytics capability uplift to support intelligence-led biosecurity targeting (CBIS) and trade market intelligence.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Named TAP priority and enterprise data strategy; intelligence-led biosecurity and CBIS (BI-04) rely on data and statistical modelling, supporting credible analytics demand.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: DAFF is the Australian Government coordinating agency for domestic biosecurity crises and runs a 3-year Emergency Management Capability Development Program targeting maturity uplift from a 2023-24 baseline.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Support to design and run training, exercises and lessons-management under the capability development program, and facilitate the annual capability maturity assessment against the Australian Disaster Preparedness Framework.
opportunity_areas.rationale: BI-08 sets explicit maturity-increase targets and the plan cites H5 bird flu preparedness; the program is named and time-bound. Note APS Commissioning Framework may limit some delivery.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: The 4-year security, technology and asset refresh aims to reduce cybersecurity threats; enterprise risk 9 covers cyber, data and critical-infrastructure security under the PSPF.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Security assessment and assurance of critical export/biosecurity systems and data as part of the refresh, aligned to the PSPF, plus cyber advisory on technical-debt remediation.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The refresh explicitly targets cybersecurity risk reduction and enterprise risk 9 names cyber expertise as a mitigation — credible but not yet a named procurement.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: An ANAO audit examined DAFF's cultural reform; the TAP includes governance enhancement and operating-model updates.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Change management and organisational-development support for cultural reform, plus workforce planning aligned to the Workforce and Recruitment Strategies 2024-27.
opportunity_areas.rationale: ANAO cultural-reform audit and TAP workforce/culture actions ('in progress') signal demand; however the Strategic Commissioning Framework and in-house preference temper the opportunity.

recommended_actions.action: Approach the Data and Digital Committee / CIO area and the PMO governing the digital roadmap with a tailored capability statement on systems delivery plus independent delivery assurance for the two flagship programs and the asset/security refresh.
recommended_actions.rationale: These are named, funded, multi-year programs with explicit governance and DTA/Finance reporting — the largest and most time-bound opportunities, and the firm can both build and assure.

recommended_actions.action: Prepare a short thought piece on transparent, defensible cost-recovery/charging and offer a financial-sustainability/ABC review to the CFO and Finance and Performance Committee.
recommended_actions.rationale: The 2024 ANAO cost-recovery audit, AMIC's call for an independent audit, and SG-02's 2026-27 review window converge into a clear near-term need to rebuild stakeholder confidence.

recommended_actions.action: Pitch an enterprise evaluation framework plus a flagship evaluation (FDF or Climate-Smart Agriculture) to the strategic-policy/evaluation function leading the TAP evaluation action.
recommended_actions.rationale: Embedding enterprise evaluation is an explicit 2025-26 TAP priority with MEL work already underway on named, large programs — a well-defined entry point.

recommended_actions.action: Track the ANAO portfolio audit pipeline, the biosecurity levy legislation's status, and Senate Estimates (Rural & Regional Affairs and Transport) for emerging assurance and review triggers.
recommended_actions.rationale: External pressure (levy controversy, cost-recovery, culture, travel-compliance audits) often crystallises into procurements; monitoring lets the firm time approaches.

recommended_actions.action: Monitor the Secretary transition and any resulting re-prioritisation before committing senior relationship effort.
recommended_actions.rationale: Leadership turnover can reset priorities; aligning pitches to the incoming leadership's agenda improves conversion.

recommended_actions.action: Offer a PGPA-aligned performance-measure health check to the Finance and Performance Committee / enterprise performance team.
recommended_actions.rationale: The major measure consolidation and several provisional/qualitative measures signal a low-cost, high-credibility entry engagement.

engagement_targets: Chief Information Officer / Data and Digital Committee and the digital roadmap PMO
Chief Financial Officer and the Finance and Performance Committee (cost recovery, financial-management reform)
Chief Risk Officer and the Audit and Risk Committee (assurance, enterprise risk)
Strategic policy / enterprise performance and evaluation function leading the TAP evaluation and performance actions
Biosecurity group leadership and the Emergency Management Capability Development Program owners
Trade and International Division (export systems, market access, eCert)
People and Culture Committee (workforce strategy, cultural reform)
talking_points: You have two named flagship system programs and a 4-year ICT refresh under a 10-year roadmap — we can both build the systems and provide independent, gateway-style delivery assurance to de-risk them.

With an ANAO cost-recovery audit and industry calling for an independent review of expenses, a transparent activity-based costing and financial-sustainability review can rebuild stakeholder confidence ahead of your 2026-27 levies-cost review.
Embedding enterprise evaluation is a 2025-26 priority — we can design a PGPA-aligned framework and evaluate flagship programs like the Future Drought Fund and Climate-Smart Agriculture.
Several of your measures are new, qualitative or 'to be reviewed' — a focused performance-measure redesign can move you toward the balanced, outcome-oriented mix your plan commits to.
Intelligence-led biosecurity and CBIS depend on data — we can accelerate your enterprise data strategy with BI and analytics capability uplift.

We understand the Strategic Commissioning Framework limits core-work outsourcing, so our offers are specialised, time-bound advisory, assurance and build engagements that lift in-house capability rather than replace it.
caveats: The plan is detailed and rich in signals, giving high confidence on the digital, cost-recovery and evaluation opportunities. External research was constrained by a search-tool limit: ANAO items are confirmed audit titles only (specific findings/recommendations not verified), and no parliamentary items were confirmed — both warrant a deeper pass. The APS Strategic Commissioning Framework explicitly targets reduced outsourcing in ICT, finance, data/research and program management, which is a material constraint on several mapped opportunities; offers should be positioned as specialised, capability-building and time-bound. Secretary turnover (per external reporting) adds near-term strategic-direction uncertainty.
